{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Adams Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | |nd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1.e4 Nh6 |eco=[[Chess/ECOB|B00]] |parent=[[Chess/King's Pawn Opening|King's Pawn Opening]] }} = Adams Defence = ===1...Nh6?!=== The Adams Defense is considered a weak response to the King's Pawn Opening, as it develops the Knight to an inferior square, allows White quick and easy development, and opens no lines for developing Black's pieces. Therefore, the Adams Defense is very uncommon. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} '''1. e4 Nh6?!'''
